{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_396dff92679a4351898197c3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tuse anyhow::Result;\n2\tuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\n3\tuse rusqlite::{params, Transaction};\n4\t\n5\t/// Result of a claim operation\n6\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n7\tpub struct ClaimResult {\n8\t    pub bead_id: String,\n9\t    pub reclaimed: usize,\n10\t}\n11\t\n12\t/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n13\t///\n14\t/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n15\t/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n16\t/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n17\t/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n18\t/// 4. Insert an event\n19\t/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n20\t/// 6. Commit\n21\t///\n22\t/// # Arguments\n23\t/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n24\t/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n25\t/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n26\t/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n27\t///\n28\t/// # Returns\n29\t/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n30\t/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n31\t/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\n32\tpub fn claim(\n33\t    tx: &Transaction,\n34\t    worker: &str,\n35\t    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n36\t    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n37\t) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n38\t    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n39\t    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n40\t    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n41\t        \"UPDATE issues\n42\t         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n43\t         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n44\t           AND updated_at < ?\",\n45\t        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n46\t    )?;\n47\t\n48\t    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n49\t    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n50\t    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n51\t    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n52\t    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n53\t        \"SELECT i.id,\n54\t                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n55\t                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n56\t                i.priority\n57\t         FROM issues i\n58\t         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n59\t         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n60\t         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n61\t           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n62\t           AND i.pinned = 0\n63\t           AND i.is_template = 0\n64\t           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n65\t         GROUP BY i.id\n66\t         ORDER BY\n67\t             downstream_impact DESC,\n68\t             critical_float ASC,\n69\t             i.priority ASC,\n70\t             i.created_at ASC\n71\t         LIMIT 1\",\n72\t    )?;\n73\t\n74\t    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n75\t\n76\t    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n77\t        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n78\t\n79\t        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n80\t        tx.execute(\n81\t            \"UPDATE issues\n82\t             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n83\t             WHERE id = ?\",\n84\t            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n85\t        )?;\n86\t\n87\t        // Step 4: Insert event\n88\t        tx.execute(\n89\t            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n90\t             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', NULL, ?, ?)\",\n91\t            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n92\t        )?;\n93\t\n94\t        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n95\t        tx.execute(\n96\t            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n97\t             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n98\t            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n99\t        )?;\n100\t\n101\t        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n102\t            bead_id,\n103\t            reclaimed,\n104\t        }))\n105\t    } else {\n106\t        Ok(None)\n107\t    }\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\t#[cfg(test)]\n111\tmod tests {\n112\t    use super::*;\n113\t    use crate::storage::Storage;\n114\t    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n115\t\n116\t    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n117\t        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n118\t        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n119\t        (temp_file, storage)\n120\t    }\n121\t\n122\t    #[test]\n123\t    fn test_claim_basic() {\n124\t        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n125\t\n126\t        // Create an open bead\n127\t        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n128\t        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n129\t\n130\t        // Claim it\n131\t        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n132\t            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n133\t        }).unwrap();\n134\t\n135\t        assert!(result.is_some());\n136\t        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n137\t        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n138\t        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n139\t\n140\t        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n141\t        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n142\t        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n143\t        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n144\t    }\n145\t\n146\t    #[test]\n147\t    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n148\t        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n149\t\n150\t        // No beads available\n151\t        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n152\t            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n153\t        }).unwrap();\n154\t\n155\t        assert!(result.is_none());\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    #[test]\n159\t    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n160\t        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n161\t\n162\t        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n163\t        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n164\t        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n165\t        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n166\t        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n167\t        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n168\t\n169\t        // Create an open bead\n170\t        let issue2 = Issue::new(\"bf-open\".to_string(), \"Open bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n171\t        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n172\t\n173\t        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n174\t        let result =     // Helper to parse optional datetime strings\n408\t        let parse_opt_dt = |idx: usize| -> Result<Option<DateTime<Utc>>> {\n409\t            let s: Option<String> = row.get(idx)?;\n410\t            match s {\n411\t                None => Ok(None),\n412\t                Some(ref val) if val.is_empty() => Ok(None),\n413\t                Some(val) => Ok(Some(parse_datetime(val)?)),\n414\t }\n622\t\n623\t    pub fn search_issues(\n624\t        &self,\n625\t        query: Option<&str>,\n626\t        status: &[Status],\n627\t        issue_type: &[IssueType],\n628\t        assignee:        LEFT JOIN labels l ON i.id = l.issue_id\n644\t             WHERE i.deleted_at IS NULL\",\n645\t        );\n646\t\n647\t        let mut params = Vec::new();\n648\t        let mut param_idx = 1;\n649\t\n650\t        if let Some(q) = query {\n651\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND (i.title LIKE ?{} OR i.description LIKE ?{})\", param_idx, param_idx + 1));\n652\t            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n653\t            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n654\t            param_idx += 2;\n655\t        }\n656\t\n657\t        for s in status {\n658\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.status = ?{}\", param_idx));\n659\t            params.push(s.to_string());\n660\t            param_idx += 1;\n661\t        }\n662\t\n663\t        for t in issue_type {\n664\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.issue_type = ?{}\", param_idx));\n665\t            params.push(t.to_string());\n666\t            param_idx += 1;\n667\t        }\n668\t\n669\t        if let Some(a) = assignee {\n670\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.assignee = ?{}\", param_idx));\n671\t            params.push(a.to_string());\n672\t            param_idx += 1;\n673\t param_idx + i)\n678\t            }).collect();\n679\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND ({}) \", label_conditions.join(\" OR \")));\n680\t            for label in labels {\n681\t                params.push(label.clone());\n682\t                param_idx += 1;\n683\t            }\n684\t        }\n685\t\n686\t        if let Some(min) = priority_min {\n687\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority >= ?{}\", param_idx));\n688\t            params.push(min.to_string());\n689\t            param_idx += 1;\n690\t        }\n691\t\n692\t        if let Some(max) = priority_max {\n693\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority <= ?{}\", param_idx));\n694\t            params.push(max.to_string());\n695\t            param_idx += 1;\n696\t        }\n697\t\n698\t        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY i.priority ASC, i.created_at ASC\");\n699\t\n700\t        if limit > 0 {\n701\t            sql.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {}\", limit));\n702\t        }\n703\t\n704\t        let mut stmt = self.conn.prepare(&sql)?;\n705\t        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n706\t        let mut rows = stmt.query(param_refs.as_slice())?;\n707\t\n708\t        let mut issues = Vec::new();\n709\t        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n710\t            issues.push(self.row_to_issue(row)?);\n711\t        }\n712\t        Ok(issues)\n713\t    }\n714\t\n715\t    pub fn get_stats(&self) -> Result<Stats> {\n716\t        let total: i64 = self.conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE anomaly_audit(outcome);\n247\t\";\n248\t\n249\t/// Split a SQL script into individual statements, respecting string literals,\n250\t/// quoted identifiers, and comments.\n251\t///\n252\t/// A naive `split(';')` breaks when SQL string literals contain semicolons\n253\t/// (e.g., `INSERT INTO t(v) VALUES('a;b')`). This function uses a small state\n254\t/// machine to track whether the current position is inside:\n255\t/// - A single-quoted string literal (`'...'`, with `''` as escape)\n256\t/// - A double-quoted identifier (`\"...\"`, with `\"\"` as escape)\n257\t/// - A line comment (`-- ...`)\n258\t/// - A block comment (`/* ... */`)\n259\t///\n260\t/// Only semicolons at the top level (outside all of the above) are treated as\n261\t/// statement terminators.\n262\tfn split_sql_statements(sql: &str) -> Vec<&str> {\n263\t    let bytes = sql.as_bytes();\n264\t    let len = bytes.len();\n265\t    let mut stmts = Vec::new();\n266\t    let mut start = 0; // byte offset where the current statement begins\n267\t    let mut i = 0;\n268\t\n269\t    // State flags — at most one is true at a time.\n270\t    let mut in_single_quote = false;\n271\t    let mut in_double_quote = false;\n272\t    let mut in_line_comment = false;\n273\t    let mut in_block_comment = false;\n274\t\n275\t    while i < len {\n276\t        let b = bytes[i];\n277\t\n278\t        // --- Line comment state ---\n279\t        if in_line_comment {\n280\t            if b == b'\\n' {\n281\t                in_line_comment = false;\n282\t            }\n283\t            i += 1;\n284\t            continue;\n285\t        }\n286\t\n287\t        // --- Block comment state ---\n288\t        if in_block_comment {\n289\t            if b == b'*' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'/' {\n290\t                in_block_comment = false;\n291\t                i += 2;\n292\t            } else {\n293\t                i += 1;\n294\t            }\n295\t            continue;\n296\t        }\n297\t\n298\t        // --- Single-quoted string state ---\n299\t        if in_single_quote {\n300\t            if b == b'\\'' {\n301\t                // '' is an escaped quote inside a string literal\n302\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\\'' {\n303\t                    i += 2;\n304\t                } else {\n305\t                    in_single_quote = false;\n306\t                    i += 1;\n307\t                }\n308\t            } else {\n309\t                i += 1;\n310\t            }\n311\t            continue;\n312\t        }\n313\t\n314\t        // --- Double-quoted identifier state ---\n315\t        if in_double_quote {\n316\t            if b == b'\"' {\n317\t                if i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'\"' {\n318\t                    i += 2;\n319\t                } else {\n320\t                    in_double_quote = false;\n321\t                    i += 1;\n322\t                }\n323\t            } else {\n324\t                i += 1;\n325\t            }\n326\t            continue;\n327\t        }\n328\t\n329\t        // --- Top-level parsing ---\n330\t        if b == b'\\'' {\n331\t            in_single_quote = true;\n332\t            i += 1;\n333\t        } else if b == b'\"' {\n334\t            in_double_quote = true;\n335\t            i += 1;\n336\t        } else if b == b'-' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'-' {\n337\t            in_line_comment = true;\n338\t            i += 2;\n339\t        } else if b == b'/' && i + 1 < len && bytes[i + 1] == b'*' {\n340\t            in_block_comment = true;\n341\t            i += 2;\n342\t        } else if b == b';' {\n343\t            // Statement terminator at top level.\n344\t            let stmt = &sql[start..i];\n345\t            if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n346\t                stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n347\t            }\n348\t            start = i + 1;\n349\t            i += 1;\n350\t        } else {\n351\t            i += 1;\n352\t        }\n353\t    }\n354\t\n355\t    // Trailing statement without a final semicolon.\n356\t    if start < len {\n357\t        let stmt = &sql[start..len];\n358\t        if !stmt.trim().is_empty() {\n359\t            stmts.push(stmt.trim());\n360\t        }\n361\t    }\n362\t\n363\t    stmts\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t/// Execute multiple SQL statements separated by semicolons.\n367\tfn execute_batch(conn: &rusqlite::Connection, sql: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n368\t    for stmt in split_sql_statements(sql) {\n369\t        conn.execute(stmt, [])?;\n370\t    }\n371\t    Ok(())\n372\t}\n373\t\n374\tpub fn apply_schema(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n375\t    execute_batch(conn, SCHEMA_SQL)?;\n376\t    Ok(())\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\tpub fn ensure_wal_mode(conn: &rusqlite::Connection) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n380\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL\", [])?;\n381\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON\", [])?;\n382\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA busy_timeout = 30000\", [])?;\n383\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA cache_size = -8000\", [])?;\n384\t    conn.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL\", [])?;\n385\t    Ok(())\n386\t}\n387\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_0b564010c2a647939271cd90","session_id":"bae09ca4-db42-4647-9c0f-fb833fc9a254","uuid":"1eb17012-2364-44de-bebf-e2d90a8ed045","timestamp":"2026-04-29T23:58:53.240Z"}

It does NOT implement the sophisticated ready() logic described in NEEDLE lines 487-507.\n\n## 2. The `bf claim` Implementation\n\n**File**: `/home/coding/bead-forge/src/claim.rs`\n\nThis IS the atomic select-score-update implementation you're looking for. The `claim()` function (lines 32-108) performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n\n1. **Reclaim stale in_progress beads** (lines 38-46)\n2. **Select candidates with impact scoring** (lines 48-72) using:\n   - `downstream_impact`: Count of beads blocked by this one\n   - `critical_float`: From `critical_path_cache` table (lower = more critical)\n   - `priority`: Standard priority field\n3. **Update the winner to in_progress** (lines 79-85)\n4. **Insert an event** (lines 87-92)\n5. **Mark the bead as dirty** (lines 94-99)\n\nThe scoring query (lines 52-72):\n```sql\nSELECT i.id,\n       COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n       COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n       i.priority\nFROM issues i\nLEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id \n    AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\nLEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\nWHERE i.status = 'open'\n  AND i.ephemeral = 0\n  AND i.pinned = 0\n  AND i.is_template = 0\n  AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\nGROUP BY i.id\nORDER BY\n    downstream_impact DESC,\n    critical_float ASC,\n    i.priority ASC,\n    i.created_at ASC\nLIMIT 1\n```\n\n## 3. Scoring Logic (priority + impact + float)\n\n**File**: `/home/coding/bead-forge/src/claim.rs` (lines 49-70)\n\nThe scoring formula is:\n```\nScore = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n```\n\nOrdering:\n1. **downstream_impact DESC** - Beads blocking the most work get priority\n2. **critical_float ASC** - Lower float (more critical) beads next\n3. **priority ASC** - Standard priority (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)\n4. **created_at ASC** - FIFO tiebreaker\n\n**Schema support** in `/home/coding/bead-forge/src/storage/schema.rs`:\n- **critical_path_cache table** (lines 202-211): Stores float values for each bead\n- **Index on float** (line 211): For efficient queries\n\n## Key Findings\n\n1. Result<()> {\n    let workspace = cli.workspace.unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(\".\"));\n    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(&workspace)\n        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n    if let Some(s) = status {\n        filter.status = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM issues WHERE id = ?\", [&id])?;\n        Ok(())\n    })?;\n\n    println!(\"Deleted bead           });\n                    println!(\"{}\", output);\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    println!(\"{}\", bead_id);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        None => {\n            println!(\"No beads available to claim\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_sync(beads_dir: &PathBuf, flush_only: bool, import_only: bool) -> Result<()> {\n Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    if import_only {\n        let result = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n        println!(\"Imported {} beads\", result.imported);\n    } else if flush_only {\n        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n        println!(\"Flushed {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n    } else {\n        let count = storage.sync_to_jsonl(&jsonl_path, false)?;\n        println!(\"Synced {} beads to JSONL\", count);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_doctor(beads_dir: &PathBuf, repair: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n\n    if repair {\n        std::fs::remove_file(&db_path)?;\n        let jsonl_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.jsonl_export);\n        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n        let result = storage.sync_from_jsonl(&jsonl_path)?;\n        println!(\"Repaired database: imported {} beads from JSONL\", result.imported);\n    } else {\n        let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n        let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n        println!(\"Database is healthy: {} beads\", count);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_count(beads_dir: &PathBuf, status: Option<String>) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let count = if let Some(s) = status {\n        let filter = IssueFilter {\n            status: Some(Status::from_str(&s).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        storage.list_issues(&filter)?.len()\n    } else {\n        storage.count_issues()?\n    };\n\n    println!(\"{}\", count);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_batch(beads_dir: &PathBuf, file: Option<PathBuf>, json: Option<String>, stdin: bool) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let ops: Vec<BatchOp> = if let Some(json_str) = json {\n        serde_json::from_str(&json_str)?\n    } else if let Some(file_path) = file {\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&file_path)?;\n        serde_json::from_str(&content)?\n    } else if stdin {\n        parse_stdin()?\n    } else {\n        return Err(anyhow!(\"Must provide --file, --json, or --stdin\"));\n    };\n\n    let results = execute_batch(&storage, ops, beads_dir)?;\n\n    // Print results\n    for result in results {\n        if result.status == \"ok\" {\n            if let Some(id) = result.id {\n                println!(\"[op {}] ok: {}\", result.op, id);\n            } else {\n                println!(\"[op {}] ok\", result.op);\n            }\n        } else {\n            eprintln!(\"[op {}] error: {}\", result.op, result.error.unwrap_or_default());\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n","numLines":615,"startLine":1,"totalLines":615}}}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_7dcd0ea924674722be206848","type":"tool_result","content":"1\tuse anyhow::Result;\n2\tuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\n3\tuse rusqlite::{params, Transaction};\n4\t\n5\t/// Result of a claim operation\n6\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n7\tpub struct ClaimResult {\n8\t    pub bead_id: String,\n9\t    pub reclaimed: usize,\n10\t}\n11\t\n12\t/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n13\t///\n14\t/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n15\t/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n16\t/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n17\t/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n18\t/// 4. Insert an event\n19\t/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n20\t/// 6. Commit\n21\t///\n22\t/// # Arguments\n23\t/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n24\t/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n25\t/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n26\t/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n27\t///\n28\t/// # Returns\n29\t/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n30\t/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n31\t/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\n32\tpub fn claim(\n33\t    tx: &Transaction,\n34\t    worker: &str,\n35\t    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n36\t    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n37\t) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n38\t    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n39\t    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n40\t    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n41\t        \"UPDATE issues\n42\t         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n43\t         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n44\t           AND updated_at < ?\",\n45\t        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n46\t    )?;\n47\t\n48\t    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n49\t    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n50\t    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n51\t    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n52\t    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n53\t        \"SELECT i.id,\n54\t                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n55\t                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n56\t                i.priority\n57\t         FROM issues i\n58\t         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n59\t         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n60\t         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n61\t           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n62\t           AND i.pinned = 0\n63\t           AND i.is_template = 0\n64\t           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n65\t         GROUP BY i.id\n66\t         ORDER BY\n67\t             downstream_impact DESC,\n68\t             critical_float ASC,\n69\t             i.priority ASC,\n70\t             i.created_at ASC\n71\t         LIMIT 1\",\n72\t    )?;\n73\t\n74\t    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n75\t\n76\t    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n77\t        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n78\t\n79\t        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n80\t        tx.execute(\n81\t            \"UPDATE issues\n82\t             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n83\t             WHERE id = ?\",\n84\t            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n85\t        )?;\n86\t\n87\t        // Step 4: Insert event\n88\t        tx.execute(\n89\t            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n90\t             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', NULL, ?, ?)\",\n91\t            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n92\t        )?;\n93\t\n94\t        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n95\t        tx.execute(\n96\t            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n97\t             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n98\t            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n99\t        )?;\n100\t\n101\t        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n102\t            bead_id,\n103\t            reclaimed,\n104\t        }))\n105\t    } else {\n106\t        Ok(None)\n107\t    }\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\t#[cfg(test)]\n111\tmod tests {\n112\t    use super::*;\n113\t    use crate::storage::Storage;\n114\t    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n115\t\n116\t    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n117\t        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n118\t        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n119\t        (temp_file, storage)\n120\t    }\n121\t\n122\t    #[test]\n123\t    fn test_claim_basic() {\n124\t        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n125\t\n126\t        // Create an open bead\n127\t        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n128\t        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n129\t\n130\t        // Claim it\n131\t        let result = assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n144\t    }\n145\t\n146\t    #[test]\n147\t    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n148\t        let (_temp, mut storage) = }).unwrap();\n154\t\n155\t        assert!(result.is_none());\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    #[test]\n159\t    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n160\t        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n161\t\n162\t        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n163\t        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n164\t        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n165\t        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n166\t        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n171\t        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n172\t\n173\t        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n174\t        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n175\t            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now())\n176\t        }).unwrap();\n177\t\n178\t        assert!(result.is_some());\n179\t        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n180\t        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n181\t\n182\t        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n183\t        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n184\t        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n185\t    }\n186\t}\n187\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bae09ca4-db42-4647-9c0f-fb833fc9a254","uuid":"a4654acb-280c-4f6c-90d2-47b2ec784a59","timestamp":"2026-04-29T23:59:12.932Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/bead-forge/src/claim.rs","content":"use anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\nuse rusqlite::{params, Transaction};\n\n/// Result of a claim operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimResult {\n    pub bead_id: String,\n    pub reclaimed: usize,\n}\n\n/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n///\n/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n/// 4. Insert an event\n/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n/// 6. Commit\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim(\n    tx: &Transaction,\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n        \"UPDATE issues\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n           AND updated_at < ?\",\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n    )?;\n\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.priority\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT 1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n        tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE id = ?\",\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 4: Insert event\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', NULL, ?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n            bead_id,\n            reclaimed,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n\n    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n        (temp_file, storage)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim it\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n\n        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // No beads available\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue2 = Issue::new(\"bf-open\".to_string(), \"Open bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n\n        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n    }\n}\n","numLines":187,"startLine":1,"totalLines":187}}}

}\n\n    pub fn with_write_transaction<T, F>(&mut self, f: F) -> Result<T>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&Transaction) -> Result<T>,\n    {\n        let tx = self.conn.transaction()?;\n        match f(&tx) {\n            Ok(result) => {\n                tx.commit()?;\n                Ok(result)\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                let _ = tx.rollback();\n                Err(e)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn with_immediate_transaction<T, F>(&self, f: F) -> Result<T>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&Transaction) -> Result<T>,\n    {\n        let mut attempt = 0;\n        loop {\n            match self.conn.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE\", []) {\n                Ok(_) => {\n                    let tx = self.conn.unchecked_transaction()?;\n                    return match f(&tx) {\n                        Ok(val) => {\n                            tx.commit()?;\n                            Ok(val)\n                        }\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            let _ = tx.rollback();\n                            Err(e)\n                        }\n                    };\n                }\n                Err(e) if is_busy_error(&e) && attempt < MAX_RETRIES => {\n                    attempt += 1;\n                    std::thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(RETRY_BASE_MS * attempt as u64));\n                    continue;\n                }\n                Err(e) => return Err(e.into()),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_issue(&self, id: &str) -> Result<Option<Issue>> {\n        let mut stmt  )?;\n            }\n\n            for dep in &issue.dependencies {\n                tx.execute(\n                    \"INSERT INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata, thread_id, created_at, created_by)\n                     VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7)\",\n                    params![\n                        &dep.issue_id,\n                        &dep.depends_on_id,\n                        &dep.dep_type.to_string(),\n                        dep.metadata.as_ref().map(|m| serde_json::to_string(m).ok()).flatten(),\n                        &dep.thread_id,\n                        &dep.created_at.to_rfc3339(),\n                        &dep.created_by,\n                    ],\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            for comment in &issue.comments {\n                tx.execute(\n                    \"INSERT INTO comments (id, issue_id, author, text, created_at)\n                     VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n                    params![\n                        &comment.id,\n                        &comment.issue_id,\n                        &comment.author,\n                        &comment.body,\n                        &comment.created_at.to_rfc3339(),\n                    ],\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn update_issue(&mut self, id: &str, changes: &IssueChanges) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            let mut updates = Vec::new();\n            let mut params: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n            if let Some(ref title) = changes.title {\n                updates.push(\"title = ?\");\n                params.push(title.clone());\n            }\n            if let Some(ref description) = changes.description {\n                updates.push(\"description = ?\");\n                params.push(description.clone());\n            }\n            if let Some(ref status) = changes.status {\n                updates.push(\"status = ?\");\n                params.push(status.to_string());\n            }\n            if let Some(priority) = changes.priority {\n                updates.push(\"priority = ?\");\n                params.push(priority.to_string());\n            }\n            if let Some(ref issue_type) = changes.issue_type {\n                updates.push(\"issue_type = ?\");\n                params.push(issue_type.to_string());\n            }\n            if let Some(ref assignee) = changes.assignee {\n                updates.push(\"assignee = ?\");\n                params.push(assignee.clone());\n            }\n\n            if !updates.is_empty() {\n                updates.push(\"updated_at = ?\");\n                let now = Utc::now().to_rfc3339();\n                params.push(now);\n\n                let query = format!(\"UPDATE issues SET {} WHERE id = ?\", updates.join(\", \"));\n                let mut all_params = params.clone();\n                all_params.push(id.to_string());\n\n                let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> =\n                    all_params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n                tx.execute(&query, param_refs.as_slice())?;\n            }\n\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn close_issue(&mut self, id: &str, reason: &str, actor: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            let now = Utc::now();\n            tx.execute(\n                \"UPDATE issues SET status = 'closed', closed_at = ?, close_reason = ?, updated_at = ?\n                 WHERE id = ?\",\n                params![now.to_rfc3339(), reason, now.to_rfc3339(), id],\n            )?;\n\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, old_value, new_value, created_at)\n                 VALUES (?1, 'closed', ?2, NULL, ?3, ?4)\",\n                params![id, actor, reason, now.to_rfc3339()],\n            )?;\n\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn count_issues(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let count: i64 = self.conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n        Ok(count as usize)\n    }\n\n    pub fn sync_from_jsonl(&mut self, jsonl_path: &Path) -> Result<ImportResult> {\n        import_jsonl(jsonl_path, |issue| {\n            let existing = self.get_issue(&issue.id)?;\n            match existing {\n                None => {\n                    self.create_issue(issue)?;\n                    Ok(true)\n                }\n                Some(_) => Ok(false),\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn sync_to_jsonl(&self, jsonl_path: &Path, dirty_only: bool) -> Result<usize> {\n        if dirty_only {\n            let result = export_jsonl_dirty(\n                jsonl_path,\n                || self.list_dirty_issues(),\n                || self.clear_dirty(),\n            )?;\n            Ok(result.count)\n        } else {\n            let result = export_jsonl(jsonl_path, || self.list_all_issues())?;\n            Ok(result.count)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn row_to_issue(&self, row: &rusqlite::Row) -> Result<Issue> {\n        let status_str: String = row.get(7)?;\n        let type_str: String = row.get(9)?;\n\n        // Helper to parse optional datetime strings\n        let parse_opt_dt = |idx: usize| -> Result<Option<DateTime<Utc>>> {\n            let s: Option<String> = row.get(idx)?;\n            match s {\n                None => Ok(None),\n                Some(ref val) if val.is_empty() => Ok(None),\n                Some(val) => Ok(Some(parse_datetime(val)?)),\n            }\n        };\n\n        Ok(Issue {\n            id: row.get(0)?,\n            content_hash: row.get(1)?,\n            title: row.get(2)?,\n            description: row.get(3)?,\n            design: row.get(4)?,\n            acceptance_criteria: row.get(5)?,\n            notes: row.get(6)?,\n            status: Status::from_str(&status_str).unwrap_or(Status::Custom(status_str)),\n            priority: row.get(8)?,\n            issue_type: IssueType::from_str(&type_str).unwrap_or(IssueType::Custom(type_str)),\n            assignee: row.get(10)?,\n            owner: row.get(11)?,\n            estimated_minutes: row.get(12)?,\n            created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(13)?)?,\n            created_by: row.get(14)?,\n            updated_at: parse_datetime(row.get(15)?)?,\n            closed_at: parse_opt_dt(16)?,\n            close_reason: row.get(17)?,\n            closed_by_session: row.get(18)?,\n            due_at: parse_opt_dt(19)?,\n            defer_until: parse_opt_dt(20)?,\n            external_ref: row.get(21)?,\n            source_system: row.get(22)?,\n            source_repo: row.get(23)?,\n            deleted_at: parse_opt_dt(24)?,\n            deleted_by: row.get(25)?,\n            delete_reason: row.get(26)?,\n            original_type: row.get(27)?,\n            compaction_level: row.get(28)?,\n            compacted_at: parse_opt_dt(29)?,\n            compacted_at_commit: row.get(30)?,\n            original_size: row.get(31)?,\n            sender: row.get(32)?,\n            ephemeral: row.get::<_, i32>(33)? != 0,\n            pinned: row.get::<_, i32>(34)? != 0,\n            is_template: row.get::<_, i32>(35)? != 0,\n            labels: self.load_labels(&row.get::<_, String>(0)?)?,\n            dependencies: self.load_dependencies(&row.get::<_, String>(0)?)?,\n            comments: self.load_comments(&row.get::<_, String>(0)?)?,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn load_labels(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<String>> {\n        let mut stmt = self.conn.prepare(\"SELECT label FROM labels WHERE issue_id = ?1\")?;\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n        let mut labels = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            labels.push(row.get(0)?);\n        }\n        Ok(labels)\n    }\n\n    fn load_dependencies(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Dependency>> {\n        let mut stmt = self.conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT issue_id, depends_on_id, type, metadata, thread_id, created_at, created_by\n             FROM dependencies WHERE issue_id = ?1\",\n        )?;\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n        let mut deps = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            let type_str: String = row.get(2)?;\n            deps.push(Dependency {\n                issue_id: row.get(0)?,\n                depends_on_id: row.get(1)?,\n                dep_type: DependencyType::from_str(&type_str).unwrap_or(DependencyType::Custom(type_str)),\n                metadata: row.get::<_, Option<String>>(3)?\n                    .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str(&s).ok()),\n                thread_id: row.get(4)?,\n                created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(5)?)?,\n                created_by: row.get(6)?,\n            });\n        }\n        Ok(deps)\n    }\n\n    fn load_comments(&self, issue_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Comment>> {\n        let mut stmt = self.conn.prepare(\n            \"SELECT id, issue_id, author, text, created_at FROM comments WHERE issue_id = ?1\",\n        )?;\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(params![issue_id])?;\n        let mut comments = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            comments.push(Comment {\n                id: row.get(0)?,\n                issue_id: row.get(1)?,\n                author: row.get(2)?,\n                body: row.get(3)?,\n                created_at: parse_datetime(row.get(4)?)?,\n            });\n        }\n        Ok(comments)\n    }\n\n    pub fn add_dependency(\n        &mut self,\n        issue_id: &str,\n        depends_on_id: &str,\n        dep_type: &DependencyType,\n        created_by: &str,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            let now = Utc::now();\n            tx.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on_id, type, created_at, created_by)\n                 VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)\",\n                params![\n )?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn remove_dependency(&mut self, issue_id: &str, depends_on_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO labels (issue_id, label) VALUES (?1, ?2)\",\n                params![issue_id, label],\n            )?;\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn remove_label(&self, ?4)\",\n                params![issue_id, author, body, now.to_rfc3339()],\n            )?;\n            Ok(tx.last_insert_rowid())\n        })\n    }\n\n    pub fn list_comments(&self, issue_id: &[IssueType],\n        assignee: Option<&str>,\n        labels: &[String],\n        priority_min: Option<i32>,\n        priority_max: Option<i32>,\n        limit: usize,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<Issue>> {\n        let mut sql = String::from(\n            \"SELECT DISTINCT i.id, i.content_hash, i.title, i.description, i.design, i.acceptance_criteria, i.notes,\n                    i.status, i.priority, i.issue_type, i.assignee, i.owner, i.estimated_minutes,\n                    i.created_at, i.created_by, i.updated_at, i.closed_at, i.close_reason,\n                    i.closed_by_session, i.due_at, i.defer_until, i.external_ref, i.source_system,\n                    i.source_repo, i.deleted_at, i.deleted_by, i.delete_reason, i.original_type,\n                    i.compaction_level, i.compacted_at, i.compacted_at_commit, i.original_size,\n                    i.sender, i.ephemeral, i.pinned, i.is_template\n             FROM issues i\n             LEFT JOIN labels l ON i.id = l.issue_id\n             WHERE i.deleted_at IS NULL\",\n        );\n\n        let mut params = Vec::new();\n        let mut param_idx = 1;\n\n        if let Some(q) = query {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND (i.title LIKE ?{} OR i.description LIKE ?{})\", param_idx, param_idx + 1));\n            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n            params.push(format!(\"%{}%\", q));\n            param_idx += 2;\n        }\n\n        for s in status {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.status = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(s.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n\n        for t in issue_type {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.issue_type = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(t.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n\n        if let Some(a) = assignee {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.assignee = ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(a.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n\n        if !labels.is_empty() {\n            let label_conditions: Vec<String> = labels.iter().enumerate().map(|(i, _)| {\n                format!(\"l.label = ?{}\", param_idx + i)\n            }).collect();\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND ({}) \", label_conditions.join(\" OR \")));\n            for label in labels {\n                params.push(label.clone());\n                param_idx += 1;\n            }\n        }\n\n        if let Some(min) = priority_min {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority >= ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(min.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n\n        if let Some(max) = priority_max {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" AND i.priority <= ?{}\", param_idx));\n            params.push(max.to_string());\n            param_idx += 1;\n        }\n\n        sql.push_str(\" ORDER BY i.priority ASC, i.created_at ASC\");\n\n        if limit > 0 {\n            sql.push_str(&format!(\" LIMIT {}\", limit));\n        }\n\n        let mut stmt = self.conn.prepare(&sql)?;\n        let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::ToSql> = params.iter().map(|p| p as &dyn rusqlite::ToSql).collect();\n        let mut rows = stmt.query(param_refs.as_slice())?;\n\n        let mut issues = Vec::new();\n        while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n            issues.push(self.row_to_issue(row)?);\n        }\n        Ok(issues)\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_stats(&self) -> Result<Stats> {\n        let total: i64 = self.conn.query_row(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE deleted_at IS NULL\", [], |row| row.get(0))?;\n\n        let open: i64 = self.conn.query_row(\n            \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE status = 'open' AND deleted_at IS NULL\",\n            [],\n            |row| row.get(0),\n        )?;\n\n        let in_progress: i64 = self.conn.query_row(\n            \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE status = 'in_progress' AND deleted_at IS NULL\",\n            [],\n            |row| row.get(0),\n        )?;\n\n        let closed: i64 = self.conn.query_row(\n            \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE status = 'closed' AND deleted_at IS NULL\",\n            [],\n            |row| row.get(0),\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Stats {\n            total: total as usize,\n            open: open as usize,\n            in_progress: in_progress as usize,\n            closed: closed as usize,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct Stats {\n    pub total: usize,\n    pub open: usize,\n    pub in_progress: usize,\n    pub closed: usize,\n}\n\nfn is_busy_error(e: &rusqlite::Error) -> bool {\n    matches!(\n        e,\n        rusqlite::Error::SqliteFailure(\n            rusqlite::ffi::Error { code: rusqlite::ErrorCode::DatabaseBusy, .. },\n            _\n        )\n    )\n}\n\nfn parse_datetime(s: String) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>> {\n Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n/// 4. Insert an event\n/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n/// 6. Commit\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim(\n    tx: &Transaction,\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n        \"UPDATE issues\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n           AND updated_at < ?\",\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n    )?;\n\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.priority\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT 1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n        tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE id = ?\",\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 4: Insert event\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', NULL, ?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n            bead_id,\n            reclaimed,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n\n    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n        (temp_file, storage)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim it\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n\n        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // No beads available\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue2 = Issue::new(\"bf-open\".to_string(), \"Open bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n\n        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":1,"oldLines":6,"newStart":1,"newLines":7,"lines":[" use anyhow::Result;"," use chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};"," use {","originalFile":"use anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Duration, Utc};\nuse rusqlite::{params, Transaction};\nuse serde::Serialize;\n\n/// Result of a claim operation\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ClaimResult {\n    pub bead_id: String,\n    pub reclaimed: usize,\n}\n\n/// A bead with its score for ready/claim operations\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct ScoredBead {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub title: String,\n    pub status: String,\n    pub priority: i32,\n    pub downstream_impact: i64,\n    pub critical_float: f64,\n    pub created_at: String,\n}\n\n/// Atomically claim a bead for a worker.\n///\n/// This function performs the following in a single IMMEDIATE transaction:\n/// 1. Reclaim stale in_progress beads (older than claim_ttl_minutes) back to open\n/// 2. Select candidates with downstream_impact + critical_float scoring\n/// 3. Update the winner to in_progress with assignee=worker\n/// 4. Insert an event\n/// 5. Mark the bead as dirty\n/// 6. Commit\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `tx` - The transaction to use (must be an IMMEDIATE transaction)\n/// * `worker` - The worker ID claiming the bead\n/// * `claim_ttl_minutes` - TTL in minutes after which in_progress beads are reclaimed\n/// * `now` - Current timestamp for staleness calculation\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(Some(claim_result))` - A bead was claimed\n/// * `Ok(None)` - No beads available to claim\n/// * `Err(e)` - Transaction error\npub fn claim(\n    tx: &Transaction,\n    worker: &str,\n    claim_ttl_minutes: i64,\n    now: DateTime<Utc>,\n) -> Result<Option<ClaimResult>> {\n    // Step 1: Reclaim stale in_progress beads\n    let stale_cutoff = now - Duration::minutes(claim_ttl_minutes);\n    let reclaimed = tx.execute(\n        \"UPDATE issues\n         SET status = 'open', assignee = NULL, updated_at = ?\n         WHERE status = 'in_progress'\n           AND updated_at < ?\",\n        params![now.to_rfc3339(), stale_cutoff.to_rfc3339()],\n    )?;\n\n    // Step 2: Find candidate beads with impact scoring\n    // Score = downstream_impact + (critical_float / 1000.0)\n    // downstream_impact = count of beads blocked by this one\n    // critical_float = from critical_path_cache (lower is more critical)\n    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(\n        \"SELECT i.id,\n                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,\n                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,\n                i.priority\n         FROM issues i\n         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')\n         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id\n         WHERE i.status = 'open'\n           AND i.ephemeral = 0\n           AND i.pinned = 0\n           AND i.is_template = 0\n           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL\n         GROUP BY i.id\n         ORDER BY\n             downstream_impact DESC,\n             critical_float ASC,\n             i.priority ASC,\n             i.created_at ASC\n         LIMIT 1\",\n    )?;\n\n    let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;\n\n    if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {\n        let bead_id: String = row.get(0)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Update the winner to in_progress\n        tx.execute(\n            \"UPDATE issues\n             SET status = 'in_progress', assignee = ?, updated_at = ?\n             WHERE id = ?\",\n            params![worker, now.to_rfc3339(), &bead_id],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 4: Insert event\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT INTO events (issue_id, event_type, actor, new_value, created_at)\n             VALUES (?, 'assignee_changed', NULL, ?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, worker, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        // Step 5: Mark as dirty\n        tx.execute(\n            \"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dirty_issues (issue_id, marked_at)\n             VALUES (?, ?)\",\n            params![&bead_id, now.to_rfc3339()],\n        )?;\n\n        Ok(Some(ClaimResult {\n            bead_id,\n            reclaimed,\n        }))\n    } else {\n        Ok(None)\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::storage::Storage;\n    use crate::model::{Issue, Status};\n\n    fn setup_test_db() -> (tempfile::NamedTempFile, Storage) {\n        let temp_file = tempfile::NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();\n        let storage = Storage::open(temp_file.path()).unwrap();\n        (temp_file, storage)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_basic() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue = Issue::new(\"bf-test1\".to_string(), \"Test bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim it\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.bead_id, \"bf-test1\");\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 0);\n\n        // Verify the bead is now in_progress\n        let updated = storage.get_issue(\"bf-test1\").unwrap().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(updated.status, Status::InProgress);\n        assert_eq!(updated.assignee.as_ref().unwrap(), \"worker1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_no_candidates() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // No beads available\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker1\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claim_reclaims_stale() {\n        let (_temp, mut storage) = setup_test_db();\n\n        // Create an in_progress bead with old updated_at\n        let mut issue = Issue::new(\"bf-stale\".to_string(), \"Stale bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        issue.status = Status::InProgress;\n        issue.assignee = Some(\"worker_old\".to_string());\n        issue.updated_at = Utc::now() - Duration::minutes(60);\n        storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an open bead\n        let issue2 = Issue::new(\"bf-open\".to_string(), \"Open bead\".to_string(), \".\".to_string());\n        storage.create_issue(&issue2).unwrap();\n\n        // Claim with 30 min TTL - should reclaim the stale one\n        let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n            claim(tx, \"worker_new\", 30, Utc::now())\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.is_some());\n        let claim_result = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(claim_result.reclaimed, 1);\n\n        // The open bead should be claimed (not the stale one, since it was reclaimed to open)\n        // After reclaim, the stale bead is open again, so it could be claimed too\n        // But the open bead has priority by created_at order\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":120,"oldLines":6,"newStart":120,"newLines":62,"lines":["     }"," }"," ","+/// Get ready candidates using the same scoring logic as claim().","+///","+/// This returns a list of beads that would be considered for claiming,","+/// ordered by the same scoring formula:","+/// - downstream_impact DESC (more blocking = higher priority)","+/// - critical_float ASC (lower float = more critical)","+/// - priority ASC (0=Critical, 4=Backlog)","+/// - created_at ASC (FIFO tiebreaker)","+///","+/// # Arguments","+/// * `tx` - The transaction to use","+/// * `limit` - Maximum number of candidates to return","+///","+/// # Returns","+/// * `Ok(Vec<ScoredBead>)` - List of scored bead candidates","+pub fn get_ready_candidates(tx: &Transaction, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<ScoredBead>> {","+    let mut stmt = tx.prepare(","+        \"SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status, i.priority,","+                COALESCE(COUNT(d.issue_id), 0) as downstream_impact,","+                COALESCE(c.float, 999999) as critical_float,","+                i.created_at","+         FROM issues i","+         LEFT JOIN dependencies d ON d.depends_on_id = i.id AND d.type IN ('blocks', 'parent-child', 'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for')","+         LEFT JOIN critical_path_cache c ON c.bead_id = i.id","+         WHERE i.status = 'open'","+           AND i.ephemeral = 0","+           AND i.pinned = 0","+           AND i.is_template = 0","+           AND i.deleted_at IS NULL","+         GROUP BY i.id","+         ORDER BY","+             downstream_impact DESC,","+             critical_float ASC,","+             i.priority ASC,","+             i.created_at ASC","+         LIMIT ?1\",","+    )?;","+","+    let mut rows = stmt.query(params![limit as i64])?;","+    let mut candidates = Vec::new();","+","+    while let Some(row) = rows.next()? {","+        candidates.push(ScoredBead {","+            id: row.get(0)?,","+            title: row.get(1)?,","+            status: row.get(2)?,","+            priority: row.get(3)?,","+            downstream_impact: row.get(4)?,","+            critical_float: row.get(5)?,","+            created_at: row.get(6)?,","+        });","+    }","+","+    Ok(candidates)","+}","+"," #[cfg(test)]"," mod tests {","     use super::*;"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

repair: bool,\n    },\n\n    /// Count beads\n    Count {\n        /// Filter by status\n        #[arg(long)]\n        status: Option<String>,\n    },\n\n    /// Batch operations (atomic)\n    Batch {\n        /// JSON file containing operations\n        #[arg(long)]\n        file: Option<PathBuf>,\n\n        /// JSON string containing operations\n        #[arg(long)]\n        json: Option<String>,\n\n        /// Read from stdin\n        #[arg(long, default_value = \"false\")]\n        stdin: bool,\n    },\n}\n\npub fn run_cli() -> Result<Cli> {\n    Ok(Cli::try_parse()?)\n}\n\npub fn run(cli: Cli) -> Result<()> {\n    let workspace = cli.workspace.unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from(\".\"));\n    let beads_dir = find_beads_dir(&workspace)\n        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\", workspace))?;\n\n    match cli.command {\n        Commands::Init { prefix } => cmd_init(&beads_dir, &prefix),\n        Commands::Create { title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label } => {\n            cmd_create(&beads_dir, title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label)\n        }\n        Commands::List { status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, format } => {\n            cmd_list(&beads_dir, status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Show { id, format } => cmd_show(&beads_dir, &id, &format),\n        Commands::Update { id, title, status, priority, assignee } => {\n            cmd_update(&beads_dir, &id, title, status, priority, assignee)\n        }\n        Commands::Close { id, reason } => cmd_close(&beads_dir, &id, &reason),\n        Commands::Reopen { id } => cmd_reopen(&beads_dir, &id),\n        Commands::Delete { id } => cmd_delete(&beads_dir, &id),\n        Commands::Ready { limit, format } => cmd_ready(&beads_dir, limit, &format),\n        Commands::Claim { assignee, model, harness, harness_version, format } => {\n            cmd_claim(&beads_dir, &assignee, model, harness, harness_version, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Sync { flush_only, import_only } => cmd_sync(&beads_dir, flush_only, import_only),\n        Commands::Doctor { repair } => cmd_doctor(&beads_dir, repair),\n        Commands::Count { status } => cmd_count(&beads_dir, status),\n        Commands::Batch { file, json, stdin } => cmd_batch(&beads_dir, file, json, stdin),\n    }\n}\n\nfn cmd_init(beads_dir: &PathBuf, prefix: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    std::fs::create_dir_all(beads_dir)?;\n\n    let config_path = beads_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n    if !config_path.exists() {\n        let config = format!(\n            r#\"issue_prefixes: [{}]\ndefault_priority: 2\ndefault_type: task\nclaim_ttl_minutes: 30\n\"#,\n            prefix\n        );\n        std::fs::write(&config_path, config)?;\n    }\n\n    let metadata_path = beads_dir.join(\"metadata.json\");\n    if !metadata_path.exists() {\n        let metadata = r#\"{\"database\": \"beads.db\", \"jsonl_export\": \"issues.jsonl\"}\"#;\n        std::fs::write(&metadata_path, metadata)?;\n    }\n\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(\"beads.db\");\n    let _storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let gitignore_path = beads_dir.join(\".gitignore\");\n    if !gitignore_path.exists() {\n        std::fs::write(&gitignore_path, \"beads.db\\nbeads.db-shm\\nbeads.db-wal\\n\")?;\n    }\n\n    println!(\"Initialized bead-forge workspace in {:?}\", beads_dir);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_create(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    title: String,\n    type_: String,\n    priority: i32,\n    description: Option<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    labels: Vec<String>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n    let prefix = get_default_prefix(&config);\n    let id = crate::id::generate_id(prefix, count);\n\n    let now = Utc::now();\n    let mut issue = Issue::new(id.clone(), title, \".\".to_string());\n    issue.issue_type = IssueType::from_str(type_.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;\n    issue.priority = Priority(priority);\n    issue.description = description;\n    issue.assignee = assignee;\n    issue.labels = labels;\n\n    storage.create_issue(&issue)?;\n\n    println!(\"{}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_list(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    status: Option<String>,\n    type_: Option<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    priority: Option<i32>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n    if let Some(s) = status {\n        filter.status = Some(Status::from_str(s.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n    }\n    if let Some(t) = type_ {\n        filter.issue_type = Some(IssueType::from_str(t.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n    }\n    filter.assignee = assignee;\n    filter.priority = priority;\n    filter.limit = limit;\n\n    let issues = storage.list_issues(&filter)?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&issue)?);\n            }\n        }\n        _ => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"[{}] {} - {} ({})\", issue.id, issue.title, issue.status, issue.priority);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"No .beads directory found in {:?}\", workspace))?;\n\n    match cli.command {\n        Commands::Init { prefix } => cmd_init(&beads_dir, &prefix),\n        Commands::Create { title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label } => {\n            cmd_create(&beads_dir, title, type_, priority, description, assignee, label)\n        }\n        Commands::List { status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, format } => {\n            cmd_list(&beads_dir, status, type_, assignee, priority, limit, &format)\n        }\n        Commands::Show { id, format } =>     Commands::Close { id, reason } => cmd_close(&beads_dir, &id, &reason),\n        Commands::Reopen { id } => cmd_reopen(&beads_dir, &id),\n        Commands::Delete { id } => cmd_delete(&beads_dir, Commands::Doctor { repair } => cmd_doctor(&beads_dir, repair),\n        Commands::Count { status } => cmd_count(&beads_dir, status),\n        Commands::Batch { file, json, stdin } => cmd_batch(&beads_dir, file, json, stdin),\n    }\n}\n\nfn cmd_init(beads_dir: &PathBuf, prefix: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    std::fs::create_dir_all(beads_dir)?;\n\n    let config_path = beads_dir.join(\"config.yaml\");\n    if !config_path.exists() {\n        let config = format!(\n            r#\"issue_prefixes: [{}]\ndefault_priority: 2\ndefault_type: task\nclaim_ttl_minutes: 30\n\"#,\n            prefix\n        );\n        std::fs::write(&config_path, config)?;\n    }\n\n    let metadata_path = beads_dir.join(\"metadata.json\");\n    if !metadata_path.exists() {\n        let metadata = r#\"{\"database\": \"beads.db\", \"jsonl_export\": \"issues.jsonl\"}\"#;\n        std::fs::write(&metadata_path, metadata)?;\n    }\n\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(\"beads.db\");\n load_config(beads_dir)?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let count = storage.count_issues()?;\n    let prefix = get_default_prefix(&config);\n    let id = crate::id::generate_id(prefix, count);\n\n    let now = Utc::now();\n    let mut issue = Issue::new(id.clone(), title, \".\".to_string());\n    issue.issue_type = IssueType::from_str(type_.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?;\n    issue.priority = Priority(priority);\n    issue.description = description;\n    issue.assignee = assignee;\n    issue.labels = labels;\n\n    storage.create_issue(&issue)?;\n\n    println!(\"{}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_list(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    status: Option<String>,\n    type_: Option<String>,\n    assignee: Option<String>,\n    priority: Option<i32>,\n    limit: Option<usize>,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n    if let Some(s) = status {\n        filter.status = Some(Status::from_str(s.as_str()).map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))?);\n    }\n    if let Some(t) = type_ {\n        filter.issue_type = storage.list_issues(&filter)?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string(&issue)?);\n            }\n        }\n        _ => {\n            for issue in issues {\n                println!(\"[{}] {} - {} ({})\", issue.id, issue.title, issue.status, issue.priority);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_show(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let issue = storage.get_issue(id)?.ok_or_else(|| anyhow!(\"Bead not found: {}\", id))?;\n\n    match format {\n        \"json\" => {\n            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&issue)?);\n        }\n        _ => {\n            println!(\"ID: {}\", issue.id);\n            println!(\"Title: {}\", issue.title);\n &issue.description {\n                println!(\"Description: {}\", desc);\n            }\n            if let Some(assignee) = &issue.assignee {\n                println!(\"Assignee: {}\", assignee);\n            }\n            if !issue.labels.is_empty() {\n                println!(\"Labels: {}\", issue.labels.join(\", \"));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_reopen(beads_dir: &PathBuf, id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let changes = IssueChanges {\n        status: Some(Status::Open),\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n\n    storage.update_issue(id, &changes)?;\n beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        tx.execute(\"DELETE FROM issues WHERE id = ?\", [&id])?;\n        Ok(())\n    })?;\n\n    println!(\"Deleted bead {}\", id);\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_ready(beads_dir: &PathBuf, limit: usize, format: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let mut filter = IssueFilter::default();\n    filter.status = issue.status, issue.priority);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn cmd_claim(\n    beads_dir: &PathBuf,\n    assignee: &str,\n    _model: Option<String>,\n    _harness: Option<String>,\n    _harness_version: Option<String>,\n    format: &str,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    let config = load_config(beads_dir)?;\n    let metadata = load_metadata(beads_dir)?;\n    let db_path = beads_dir.join(&metadata.database);\n    let mut storage = Storage::open(&db_path)?;\n\n    let claim_ttl = config.claim_ttl_minutes;\n    let result = storage.with_immediate_transaction(|tx| {\n        claim(tx, assignee, claim_ttl, Utc::now())\n    })?;\n\n    match result {\n        Some(ClaimResult { bead_id, reclaimed }) => {\n            match format {\n                \"json\" => {\n
